notes.note: C. Claudius Marcellus was returned consul-designate for 50 BC at the elections of summer 51 BC (the salutation styles him cos. desig.). Cicero, then proconsul on his way to Cilicia, writes to congratulate him as soon as the news reached him.

notes.anchor: by your father's many kindnesses I have been both defended in dark hours and adorned in fair ones
notes.note: An allusion to the Claudii Marcelli's support during Cicero's exile (58-57 BC, the 'dark hours') and his subsequent honours; Cicero presents himself as a client of the whole house.
